  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Torquing rubber suspension bushings at full droop introduces severe torsional preload once the vehicle is lowered to ride height."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Loosen all affected suspension bushing fasteners.",
    "Lower the vehicle to normal ride height (curb height) or compress the suspension to ride height using a jack.",
    "Torque all bushing fasteners to OEM specification while the suspension is under normal load."
  ],
  "rationale": "Torquing rubber bushings while the suspension is at full droop locks the inner metal sleeve in a rotated position relative to the outer shell. When the vehicle is lowered to its normal ride height, the rubber is subjected to constant, excessive torsional stress, leading to rapid bushing tear, noise, and altered suspension geometry/handling.",
  "references": [
    "OEM Service Manual - Suspension Installation Procedures",
    "Standard Alignment Specifications"
  ]
}
